root_text.an4.23:2.1: Yaṁ, bhikkhave, sadevakassa lokassa samārakassa sabrahmakassa sassamaṇabrāhmaṇiyā pajāya sadevamanussāya diṭṭhaṁ sutaṁ mutaṁ viññātaṁ pattaṁ pariyesitaṁ anuvicaritaṁ manasā, sabbaṁ taṁ tathāgatena abhisambuddhaṁ.
root_text.an4.23:3.1: Yañca, bhikkhave, rattiṁ tathāgato anuttaraṁ sammāsambodhiṁ abhisambujjhati yañca rattiṁ anupādisesāya nibbānadhātuyā parinibbāyati, yaṁ etasmiṁ antare bhāsati lapati niddisati sabbaṁ taṁ tatheva hoti, no aññathā.
root_text.an4.23:4.1: Yathāvādī, bhikkhave, tathāgato tathākārī, yathākārī tathāvādī.
root_text.an4.23:5.1: Sadevake, bhikkhave, loke samārake sabrahmake sassamaṇabrāhmaṇiyā pajāya sadevamanussāya tathāgato abhibhū anabhibhūto aññadatthu daso vasavattī.

translation_text.an4.23:1.1: “Mendicants, the world has been understood by the Realized One;
translation_text.an4.23:1.2: and he is detached from the world.
translation_text.an4.23:1.3: The origin of the world has been understood by the Realized One;
translation_text.an4.23:1.4: and he has given up the origin of the world.
translation_text.an4.23:1.5: The cessation of the world has been understood by the Realized One;
translation_text.an4.23:1.6: and he has realized the cessation of the world.
translation_text.an4.23:1.7: The practice that leads to the cessation of the world has been understood by the Realized One;
translation_text.an4.23:1.8: and he has developed the practice that leads to the cessation of the world.
translation_text.an4.23:2.1: In this world—with its gods, Māras, and Divinities, this population with its ascetics and brahmins, its gods and humans—whatever is seen, heard, thought, known, attained, sought, and explored by the mind, all that has been understood by the Realized One.
translation_text.an4.23:2.2: That’s why he’s called the ‘Realized One’.
translation_text.an4.23:3.1: From the night when the Realized One awakens to the supreme perfect awakening until the night he becomes fully extinguished—in the element of extinguishment with no residue—everything he speaks, says, and expresses is real, not otherwise.
translation_text.an4.23:3.2: That’s why he’s called the ‘Realized One’.
translation_text.an4.23:4.1: The Realized One does as he says, and says as he does.
translation_text.an4.23:4.3: that’s why he’s called the ‘Realized One’.
translation_text.an4.23:5.1: In this world—with its gods, Māras, and divinities, this population with its ascetics and brahmins, gods and humans—the Realized One is the vanquisher, the unvanquished, the universal seer, the wielder of power.
translation_text.an4.23:5.2: That’s why he’s called the ‘Realized One’.
translation_text.an4.23:6.1: Directly knowing the whole world as it is,
translation_text.an4.23:6.3: he is detached from the whole world,
translation_text.an4.23:7.1: That attentive one is the champion
translation_text.an4.23:7.2: who is released from all ties.
translation_text.an4.23:7.4: extinguishment, fearing nothing from any quarter.
translation_text.an4.23:8.1: He is the Buddha, with defilements ended,
translation_text.an4.23:8.3: He has attained the end of all deeds,
translation_text.an4.23:8.4: freed with the end of attachments.
translation_text.an4.23:9.1: That Blessed One is the Buddha,
translation_text.an4.23:9.3: in all the world with its gods,
translation_text.an4.23:10.1: And so those gods and humans,
translation_text.an4.23:10.2: who have gone to the Buddha for refuge,
translation_text.an4.23:11.1: ‘Tamed, he is the best of tamers,
translation_text.an4.23:11.2: peaceful, he is the seer among the peaceful,
translation_text.an4.23:11.3: liberated, he is the foremost of liberators,
translation_text.an4.23:11.4: crossed over, he is the most excellent of guides across.’
translation_text.an4.23:12.3: In the world with its gods,

variant_text.an4.23:6.3: Sabbaṁ lokaṁ → sabbaloka (bj, sya-all, km, pts1ed)
variant_text.an4.23:12.4: natthi me → natthi te (bj, sya-all, km, pts1ed)
